rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. POPE & POPE PROPERTIES LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 2: CROSS DEFENDANTS POPE & POPE PROPERTIES LLC and JAMES POPE's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT: OFF CALENDAR, Notice of Stay of Proceeding Regarding Bankruptcy on File. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TALAL MUHAWIEH et al VS. KEN G. WEI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 3 DEFENDANTS KEN WEI and KGW ENTERPRISE LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication: GRANTED. Moving party shifted the burden and opposing party failed to produce evidence to create a triable issue of fact. D'Amico objection to Talal Declaration contradicting his prior testimony is sustained. Plaintiffs' complaint is based on allegations of fraud. Reasonable reliance is a necessary element of fraud. Reliance element is ordinarily a question of fact, but it may be decided as a matter of law "if reasonable minds can come to only one conclusion based on the facts." Guido v. Koopman (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 837. Defendants produced evidence that Plaintiff stopped trusting Maher before engaging counsel and negotiating the Forbearance Agreement. (Deposition of Talal page 106:19-20). Plaintiffs failed to produce any admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. Specifically, (1) No exhibit F was attached to Plaintiff's Volume of Evidence; (2) Declaration of Talal paragraph 18 does not support the contention asserted; and (3) Declaration of Talal paragraph 24 contradicts prior testimony and cannot be used to create a triable issue of fact under D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 22. All claims predating the Forbearance Agreement are expressly released by the Agreement. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWIN KEITH STEPHENS et al VS. CAL-WESTERN RECONVEYANCE CORP. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK's DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT: Sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege: (1) tender under the second lien; (2) fraud with specificity as to each actionable misrepresentation "how, when, who, in what manner and to whom."; (3) reasonable reliance on February 25, 2013 misrepresentation in light of opposition to TRO request on February 22, 2013; (4) damages not caused by Plaintiff's default; and (5) facts giving rise to duty and breach of duty. Request for Judicial Notice is granted. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PLACE OF FRANK'S, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. SEQUOIA MORTAGE CAPITAL INC A CALIFORNIA CORP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 8: Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden. Plaintiffs fail to sustain their burden and show a possibility of prevailing on the merits and irreparable harm. Plaintiffs do not submit competent evidence that Myers spent the money ($300,000) on construction and, but for Fried's non-cooperation, would have gotten the money from the construction Disbursement Account, and used it to pay mortgage. There are no receipts, cancelled checks, permits, DBI approvals, etc. Plaintiffs also do not produce any evidence that these repairs were done and reimbursement could have been requested before the loan went into default in December 2013. Also, there is no showing of irreparable harm. Mr. Myers admits that he wanted to sell the property for years, thus, the sole proposition in support of showing of irreparable harm, i.e. that that real property is unique, is irrelevant in this case. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ZAFIRI LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. WILLIAM SEELEY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 8, 2014, Line 9: PLAINTIFF ZAFIRI LIMITED PARTNERSHIP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: GRANTED, subject to opposition. (501/REQ)
